public int numRollsToTarget(int n, int k, int target) {
    int MOD = 1000000007;
    if (target < n || target > n * k) {
        return 0;
    }
    int[][] dp = new int[n+1][target+1];
    dp[0][0] = 1;
    for (int i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
        int maxSum = i * k;
        int upper = Math.min(maxSum, target);
        for (int j = i; j <= upper; j++) {
            for (int f = 1; f <= k; f++) {
                if (j - f >= 0) {
                    dp[i][j] += dp[i-1][j - f];
                    dp[i][j] %= MOD;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    return dp[n][target];
}

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int numRollsToTarget(int n, int k, int target) {
        final int MOD = 1000000007;
        if (target < n || target > n * k) {
            return 0;
        }
        int[][] dp = new int[n + 1][target + 1];
        dp[0][0] = 1;
        for (int i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
            int maxSum = i * k;
            int upper = Math.min(maxSum, target);
            for (int j = i; j <= upper; j++) {
                for (int f = 1; f <= k; f++) {
                    if (j - f >= 0) {
                        dp[i][j] = (dp[i][j] + dp[i - 1][j - f]) % MOD;
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return dp[n][target];
    }
}
```

efficiency: HIGH

time complexity: O(n * k * target)

space complexity: O(n * target)

energy implications: LOW

explanation: This solution uses dynamic programming to efficiently count the number of ways to achieve the target sum with the given dice. The DP table `dp[i][j]` keeps track of the number of ways to achieve sum `j` using `i` dice. The approach iterates through each die and each possible face value, updating the DP table in a bottom-up manner. The solution handles edge cases where the target is impossible to achieve and ensures results are modulo 1e9+7. The time complexity is manageable given the constraints, and the space complexity is optimized by using a 2D array.
